author: Alexander Brittain
description: Dictate notes with Whisper or Cohere Transcribe; clean up with a local Ollama model. Private, on-device speech-to-text for Obsidian. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Alexander Brittain
manifest.description: Private, on-device speech-to-text. Dictate notes with Whisper or Cohere Transcribe; clean up with a local Ollama model.
manifest.name: Local Dictation

name: Local Dictation

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

- **System audio capture.** A new "System audio" input source transcribes your computer's own audio output — meetings, calls, videos — instead of the microphone, running through the same fully local VAD → transcription pipeline. Windows loopback capture ships via WASAPI; native Linux capture works through PulseAudio/PipeWire. The dictation ribbon's level meter now reads from the sidecar's mixed signal, so it animates correctly during system-audio sessions too.
- **Speaker diarization.** Opt-in, fully local speaker labeling attributes each part of a transcript to a session-stable speaker, rendered with a `**Speaker N:**` label — no accounts, no network, and no stored voiceprints. A bundled segmentation model splits a single utterance into per-speaker turns, so rapid back-and-forth is attributed to the right speaker instead of collapsing onto one. A new setting toggles whether speaker labels are written into the transcript.

## Fixes

- **No-microphone startup is quieter and clearer.** Expected "no microphone detected" handling no longer logs as noisy errors, the startup notice reads more clearly, and Linux behaves correctly when no microphone is present.
- **Diarization failures now surface instead of being silently dropped.** A speaker-embedding failure on a turn records a `Failed` diarization stage with an error message rather than quietly skipping that turn.
- **Bundled-model attribution now ships with the sidecar.** Each sidecar release archive now includes `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md` with license text for the bundled WeSpeaker (CC BY 4.0) and pyannote segmentation (MIT) models used by diarization.
- **Existing installs migrate cleanly** to the new speaker-label setting.

## Internal

- Added an end-to-end transcription-quality test suite with a multi-speaker LibriSpeech corpus, expanded sidecar integration tests covering both system audio and diarization, and hardened the diarization clustering/accuracy/noise-robustness paths with mined confusable-speaker fixtures.
- Documented the Silero VAD license notice.
- Routine dependency maintenance: CI actions and dev-tooling groups, plus native crate bumps (libloading 0.9, criterion 0.8) and `@types/node` 26.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

- **External edits now show up when Obsidian regains focus.** Presets you change in another window, edits that arrive through sync, and local models you pull from the terminal (`ollama pull`) are picked up automatically the next time the Obsidian window gains focus — no reload or reopening settings. A new serialized preset store keeps a focus refresh, or an unrelated settings save, from ever clobbering preset edits made elsewhere.
- **Search in the preset manager.** The preset manager gains a search bar that behaves like the community plugin browser: type to filter the built-ins and your own presets by name or description, with the matched text highlighted. Empty sections collapse, and your query survives opening and closing a preset.
- **Your OpenRouter API key is now stored encrypted.** The key moves out of plaintext `data.json` and into Obsidian's encrypted Secret Storage; only an opaque secret id is persisted or synced, and the key is read only at the OpenRouter request boundary. An existing plaintext key migrates automatically on first load and the old field is stripped — the migration is idempotent, so a synced or restored `data.json` can never overwrite a newer key with a stale one. Because encryption at rest landed in Obsidian 1.11.5, this release now requires desktop **1.11.5** or newer.
- **GPU acceleration upgraded to CUDA 13.2.** The release CUDA sidecars (Windows and Linux) and their bundled runtimes now build against CUDA 13.2, with support for Visual Studio 2026. **Action for GPU users:** the CUDA sidecar now needs an NVIDIA driver compatible with CUDA 13.x (**R580 or newer**) — update your driver before upgrading if you rely on GPU acceleration.

## Fixes

- **Preset manager duplicate, delete, and save are reliable.** Clicking the pencil/copy/trash icons on a preset row no longer also opens the editor. Duplicating now picks the first free "(copy)" / "(copy N)" name so duplicate-then-save works immediately, and the save path re-adds a preset that was deleted elsewhere instead of silently dropping it. The user preset cap is raised from 25 to 50, the retired "Brain dump organizer" built-in falls back to Clean up, and a muted size estimate (~tokens / chars) now sits under the prompt so you can see how large each request will be.
- **Sidecar updates now cover every variant you installed.** A plugin update refreshes all of your installed sidecars — CPU and CUDA — instead of only the currently selected engine, through one prompt and one multi-variant flow with per-variant progress (e.g. `CUDA sidecar (1 of 2)`). The preferred runtime updates first, so a partial failure still leaves your selected engine current; development bundles and explicit sidecar-path overrides are left untouched.

## Internal

- **Much faster CUDA release builds.** Caching the extracted Windows CUDA toolkit and tightening the cache identity cut the warm CUDA build legs from ~21–23 minutes to about **2m41s (Windows)** and **3m01s (Linux)**; each release now also emits a timing summary and prunes unreachable Actions caches.
- Biome upgraded to 2.5.0 (recommended-rules preset migrated).
- Post-merge cleanup of the above changes: a regression test pins the idempotent OpenRouter migration, the duplicated `requiredEnv` env-var helper is shared from `scripts/lib/`, and a mislabeled packaging test is renamed to what it actually covers. No behavior change.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

- **Preset manager.** The inline prompt textarea, the ephemeral Custom state, and the save-style dialog are replaced by a drill-in preset manager (same pattern as Manage models): browse the built-ins, then create, edit, duplicate, rename, or delete your own presets. An existing custom prompt migrates automatically into a "My preset" entry.
- **Presets can add instead of replace.** Each preset now declares where its output goes: replace the transcript, or add generated content above/below the untouched transcript at the end of a session. The built-in lineup follows suit — **TLDR** now adds its summary above your text instead of rewriting it, a new **Action items** preset appends a checklist below, and Voice commands is retired (old references fall back to Clean up).
- **Per-preset overrides.** A preset can pin minimum words, temperature, and note-as-context; blank fields inherit your global settings. While the active preset pins a value, the corresponding panel row is locked with a "Set by preset" explanation instead of silently ignoring your input.
- **OpenRouter test button.** One click on the model row sends a minimal completion through your saved key and model id, proving key validity, credits, and the exact model end to end — a checkmark flash on success, a specific message on failure.
- **Configurable remote timeout.** Advanced > Limits gains a remote request timeout (default 60 s, 5–600 s) for OpenRouter cleanup calls, shown only when remote or auto routing is active.

## Fixes

- **An empty LLM result no longer eats your words.** If a per-utterance cleanup returns empty output, the raw utterance now stays in the note and the failure is surfaced, matching the batch path. An additive preset that produces nothing shows a quiet notice instead of looking like success.
- **Ollama status recovers on its own.** A failed model-catalog load used to mark the provider as loaded, so the focus-time recheck skipped it forever and the status stayed unhealthy until a manual refresh. Providers whose last load failed are now retried when the panel opens, the window regains focus, or routing changes.
- **Failure messages name the real culprit.** Errors carry the provider that actually handled the request (flipping the privacy kill switch mid-call can no longer misattribute a failure), "model not found" now distinguishes an unconfigured Ollama model from a missing OpenRouter id, and an Ollama missing-model 404 maps to the same clear message as OpenRouter's instead of a generic "LLM transform failed."
- **Your timing choice survives a restart.** Disabling Transform and re-enabling it later restores the mode you last chose instead of resetting it; when a preset pins the timing, the mode row stays visible but locks with an explanation.
- **Output-token caps scale with the dictated text** on every path, and the note-context/prior-utterance wrapping no longer inflates the remote billing ceiling.
- **OpenRouter picker polish.** Models that don't declare modalities are no longer hidden, and the key and catalog probes run in parallel so the status line settles about twice as fast.
- **Sidebar polish.** The auto-threshold explainer moved into the standard info tooltip, the total context cap is hidden in batch mode (it only governs per-utterance context), the Auto-tab "Local · Ollama" / "Remote · OpenRouter" labels are restyled as single-line dividers, and the note-as-context toggle is marked experimental with a note about remote input-token cost.

## Internal

- Releases now version as `YYYY.M.MICRO`; this release is 2026.6.10.
- The persisted settings shape changed: `llmPostprocessPrompt` is removed (the active preset ref is the single source of truth) and legacy per-preset `mode`/`minWords`/`temperature` fields migrate into `timing`/`overrides` on load.
- Type declarations stay resolvable under the Obsidian community scanner's TypeScript 5.4.
- Routine dependency bumps (npm dev tooling, Rust minor/patch group).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

- **Maintenance release for Obsidian community-review compliance.** No user-facing changes — every dictation, transform, and model behavior is identical to 2026.6.8. This release only adjusts how a couple of internal calls are written so the plugin clears the latest Obsidian automated-review ruleset.

## Internal

- Network requests reach `fetch` through `window.fetch` (a member access) instead of the bare global, satisfying `no-restricted-globals` without an inline eslint-disable directive. The desktop-only streamed byte-cap reads and `AbortSignal` cancellation are unchanged.
- The two remaining show/hide toggles drive visibility with a CSS class via `toggleClass('local-stt-hidden', …)` instead of assigning `element.style.display`, satisfying `obsidianmd/no-static-styles-assignment`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

- **Obsidian community-review compliance.** This is a quality-and-compliance release with no user-facing feature changes — every dictation, transform, and model behavior is identical to 2026.6.7. It brings the plugin in line with the Obsidian community-plugin review guidelines so it can pass automated review:
  - Show/hide UI state (the microphone **Detect** button and the sidecar-install settings group) now lives in a CSS class instead of inline element styles, so themes can override it.
  - The settings tab no longer renders a redundant "Local Dictation" heading at the top — the pane is already titled with the plugin name, and it now opens straight to the model card per Obsidian's settings conventions.

## Internal

- **Adopted `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` with type-aware linting.** The Obsidian review ruleset — which extends typescript-eslint's type-checked rules — now runs in the build pipeline via a new `lint:obsidian` step wired into `check:frontend`, alongside the existing Biome lint. This catches guideline and type-safety regressions before a release instead of at review time.
- Conformance fixes surfaced by the new ruleset, none of which change runtime behavior: timer calls go through `window.setTimeout`/`window.clearTimeout`, DOM creation uses Obsidian's `activeDocument`, fire-and-forget async work in lifecycle hooks and event handlers is explicitly voided, the desktop-only streaming `fetch` (needed for cancellation and the response byte cap) is documented as an intentional exemption from `requestUrl`, and a handful of unsafe casts were replaced with properly typed access.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

- **LLM cleanup is now opt-in.** LLM features are off by default — the transformation sidebar no longer bootstraps on first run, so a fresh install is pure local speech-to-text until you turn cleanup on. The Advanced toggle now reads "Enable LLM features" (positive) instead of the old double-negative "Disable LLM features". Existing vaults keep their saved setting.

## Fixes

- **The dictation cursor stays put until your words land.** Pressing Stop no longer hides the insertion cursor while queued transcripts are still draining on a slow backend — it stays where text will appear and is only released once the session finishes. With overlapping sessions, the shared cursor now belongs to the newest session, so a draining older one can't yank focus. Batch cleanup still hands the cursor over to the flashing "working" range as before.
- **Stale speech engine is detected and one-click fixable.** Obsidian updates the plugin files but never the separately-installed sidecar, so the two could silently fall out of sync after an update. On startup the plugin now compares the installed engine's version against the plugin version and, when they differ, shows a notice with a "Reinstall speech engine" action. The sidecar also reports its own real compiled version now, instead of echoing back the plugin version.

## Internal

- Corrected an inaccurate comment about the shared session-processing range in the editor surface.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

- **Microphone input device picker** — Transcription settings now have a "Microphone" row that lets you pick which input device dictation uses. Default stays the OS default mic; picked devices persist across sessions and apply on the next dictation session. Hot-plugged mics show up while the settings tab is open (devicechange-driven, 300 ms debounce). If a saved device is gone when dictation starts, capture transparently falls back to the OS default and shows a Notice instead of failing outright.
- **Ribbon: tail-hold animated state and balanced custom bars** — After speech ends, the audio-lines icon keeps animating for 5 s before settling, so micro-pauses no longer flip the icon back and forth. The bar normalization is now per-band peak AGC, so sibilants and vowels both register without one drowning out the other. Honors `prefers-reduced-motion`, and the `aria-label`/`title` follow the real controller state immediately so screen readers don't lag the visual hold.

## Performance

- **Cached session capabilities in the worker.** Model-family capabilities are resolved once at session load and reused for every utterance instead of looked up through the registry per utterance. Same gating and stage-context behavior, fewer per-utterance lookups on the hot transcription path.

## Fixes

- **Crash-safe sidecar installs and non-blocking decompress.** A killed install no longer leaves the user without a working sidecar; the installer renames the prior install to a `.old` directory, promotes staging into place, and only then removes the backup (rolling back from `.old` if promotion fails). Decompression now streams `createReadStream → createGunzip → Writable` instead of loading the whole archive and calling `gunzipSync` on the event loop — relevant now for the CPU sidecar, load-bearing for the CUDA bundle.
- **Protocol frame parser caps payload size and delivers before failing.** The TS-side frame parser now caps payloads at 16 MiB (matching the Rust side) so a flipped length header can't drive a multi-GiB allocation in the renderer, and decoded frames in a chunk are delivered before any fatal is raised so a single corrupt later byte no longer discards an already-decoded transcript frame.
- **Shutdown is a hard cancel with drained waiters.** `Command::Shutdown` is now explicitly documented and tested as a hard process-level cancel — hosts that need final transcripts should stop sessions first, wait for `session_stopped`, then terminate. On the TypeScript side, shutdown rejects all pending waiters before stopping the process so restart doesn't resolve stale requests against a replacement process, uses stdin EOF only (drops the redundant wire-level `shutdown` frame), and cancelling a stale model install no longer spawns a fresh sidecar.
- **Stage runner always reports `durationMs`.** Object stage payloads receive measured duration metadata from the runner, even when a stage didn't predeclare a placeholder — stage authors no longer have to remember the placeholder for duration to be reported.
- **Settings setup state hardened.** `PluginSettings` now carries an explicit `schemaVersion: 1`, and `setupCompletedAt` only survives resolution when it's an exact persisted ISO timestamp — corrupt or loose date strings resolve to `null` so malformed settings can't falsely mark setup as completed. Batch cleanup configured against a closed note now logs a warning instead of silently skipping.
- **Hallucination filter and Ollama postprocess are kinder to real dictation.** Prompt-leak drops now require two corroborating silence/quality signals, and bare-domain detection is constrained to recognized suffixes so `node.js` and `v1.2` are treated as normal text. Ollama output length checks and `outputChars` telemetry count characters instead of bytes, keeping multibyte text accounting correct, and truncation reporting for `done_reason: "length"` is now pinned by tests.

## Internal

- TypeScript sidecar protocol parsing slimmed to trust Rust-side event payloads — JSON object/type validation and unknown-event rejection stay at the plugin boundary, but per-field re-validation is gone (-733 net lines in `src/sidecar/protocol.ts`).
- TypeScript test suite trimmed to high-ROI behavior; new coverage added for the audio resampler and path-validation trust boundary, sidecar connection event round-trips, context-cap and sidecar reinstall paths, and shared fixtures live under `test/fixtures/`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

- **Multi-session sidecar** — the sidecar now keeps per-session model instances and routes audio, stop, and cancel by session ID, so dictation can run across multiple notes without one session stomping on another. Native batch LLM cleanup is preserved across sessions.
- **Audio-reactive ribbon** — the ribbon's audio-lines icon now moves with your voice. A lightweight tap on the capture graph splits the FFT into six log-spaced speech bands (80–8000 Hz) with attack/release smoothing, and the six bars animate while VAD reports speech. Honors `prefers-reduced-motion`.
- **Welcome-flavored setup wizard** — first-run experience is friendlier, the sidecar install modal is clearer, and Manage Models routes through the wizard when the sidecar isn't installed yet.
- **No clipboard access** — the only `navigator.clipboard` call in the plugin (the "Copy error" button in the sidecar install failure UI) was removed in response to Obsidian plugin review. Error text remains selectable in the modal.

## Fixes

- Batch LLM cleanup presets (TLDR, Markdown, Brain dump, Voice commands, custom batch presets) no longer silently drop the cleaned output on sessions with more than one utterance. The raw transcript used to be left in place with a `batch cleanup replacement skipped — session range no longer available` warning; cleanup now applies as intended.
- `npm run install:dev` no longer leaves stale optional sidecar artifacts in the dev vault when a previous build's sidecars are missing on the new build.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

- **Setup wizard** — guided first-run experience walks you through installing the sidecar, picking a model, and starting your first dictation. Manage Models now routes through the wizard when the sidecar isn't installed yet.
- **LLM transformation panel rebuild** — reordered around the natural flow (Transform → Ollama model → Preset → Mode → Note context). An inline status row under the Transform toggle surfaces Ollama health — unreachable, no models installed, selected model gone, or no model picked — instead of burying it in Diagnostics.
- **Note as LLM context toggle** — opt in to include the open note above the cursor in the LLM prompt, or keep dictation context-isolated.
- **Live hardware acceleration** — switching CPU/CUDA/Metal preference applies immediately without restarting dictation.
- **One release per version** — the plugin bundle and native sidecar archives now ship on the same GitHub release tag. Obsidian's auto-updater consumes the plugin files; the plugin's installer fetches the sidecar archive from the same tag on demand.

## Fixes

- Cursor-mode dictation no longer blocks with a "No active Markdown editor" notice when the cursor isn't focused in an editor — it appends to the end of the active note.
- Sidecar install URLs no longer route through a separate `sidecar-<version>` tag, removing the version indirection from the previous release scheme.
